message:
feat(agent-sweep): Add release workflow detection

Implements bd-y3vd: Detect if repo should have release automation
(Phase 3).

Adds two functions:
- has_release_workflow(): Check if repo has release automation
  Checks in order (first match wins):
  1. Per-repo config: .ru/agent-sweep.conf (AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY)
  2. User config: ~/.config/ru/agent-sweep.d/<repo>.conf
  3. gh API: Check remote for release/deploy/publish workflows
  4. Local files: Scan .github/workflows/*.yml for release patterns

- get_release_strategy(): Get the release strategy ("never", "auto",
  "tag-only", "gh-release") for a repo

This enables agent-sweep to determine if Phase 3 (release planning)
should run for each repository.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-koxf","title":"Define review plan artifact schema and validation","description":"# Task: Define Review Plan Artifact Schema and Validation

## Purpose
Define the machine-readable JSON schema that Claude must produce for each review session. This artifact is the contract between the Plan phase and Apply phase.

## Background: Why a Structured Artifact?
- Apply phase needs to know what to do
- Can't scrape agent transcripts reliably
- Enables resume after interruption
- Provides audit trail
- Enables metrics without parsing text

## File Location
```
{worktree}/.ru/review-plan.json
```

## Schema (Version 1)

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "worktree_path": "/home/user/.local/state/ru/worktrees/.../owner_repo",
  
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Authentication fails on Windows",
      "priority": "high",
      "decision": "fix",
      "notes": "Root cause: path separator in auth.py:234",
      "risk_level": "low",
      "files_changed": ["src/auth.py"],
      "lines_changed": 5
    },
    {
      "type": "pr",
      "number": 15,
      "title": "Add Redis caching",
      "priority": "normal",
      "decision": "skip",
      "notes": "Out of scope - adds external dependency",
      "risk_level": "n/a"
    }
  ],
  
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q1",
      "prompt": "Should I refactor all path handling or just fix this case?",
      "options": [
        {"label": "Quick fix", "description": "Fix only auth.py (5 lines)"},
        {"label": "Full refactor", "description": "Modernize all paths (45 lines)"},
        {"label": "Skip", "description": "Not a priority"}
      ],
      "recommended": "Quick fix",
      "answered": true,
      "answer": "Quick fix",
      "answered_at": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ],
  
  "git": {
    "branch": "ru/review/20250104-103000-12345/owner-repo",
    "base_ref": "main",
    "commits": [
      {
        "sha": "abc123def456",
        "subject": "Fix Windows path handling in auth.py",
        "files": ["src/auth.py"],
        "insertions": 3,
        "deletions": 2
      }
    ],
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  },
  
  "gh_actions": [
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "body": "Fixed in commit abc123. The issue was path separators on Windows..."
    },
    {
      "op": "close",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "reason": "completed"
    },
    {
      "op": "label",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]
    },
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "pr#15",
      "body": "Thank you for the suggestion. After review, this is out of scope..."
    }
  ],
  
  "metadata": {
    "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
    "completed_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
    "duration_seconds": 900,
    "context_usage_percent": 45,
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4",
    "driver": "local"
  }
}
```

## Field Definitions

### Top Level
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| schema_version | int | yes | Always 1 for this version |
| run_id | string | yes | Unique review run identifier |
| repo | string | yes | owner/repo format |
| worktree_path | string | yes | Absolute path to worktree |
| items | array | yes | Work items reviewed |
| questions | array | no | Questions asked/answered |
| git | object | no | Git state (may be empty if no changes) |
| gh_actions | array | no | GitHub actions to perform in Apply |
| metadata | object | yes | Session metadata |

### Item Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| type | "issue"\|"pr" | yes | Work item type |
| number | int | yes | Issue/PR number |
| title | string | yes | Item title |
| priority | string | yes | high/normal/low |
| decision | string | yes | fix/skip/needs-info/closed |
| notes | string | no | Explanation of decision |
| risk_level | string | no | low/medium/high/n/a |
| files_changed | array | no | Files modified for this item |
| lines_changed | int | no | Net lines changed |

### Question Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| id | string | yes | Unique question ID |
| prompt | string | yes | Question text |
| options | array | no | Available options |
| recommended | string | no | Agent's recommendation |
| answered | bool | yes | Whether answered |
| answer | string | no | Selected answer (if answered) |
| answered_at | string | no | ISO timestamp |

### gh_action Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| op | string | yes | comment/close/label/merge |
| target | string | yes | issue#N or pr#N |
| body | string | no | Comment body (for comment op) |
| reason | string | no | Close reason (for close op) |
| labels | array | no | Labels to add (for label op) |

## Validation Function
```bash
validate_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    # Must exist
    [[ ! -f "$plan_file" ]] && { echo "Plan file not found"; return 1; }
    
    # Must be valid JSON
    if ! jq empty "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Required top-level fields
    if ! jq -e '.schema_version and .repo and .items' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Missing required fields: schema_version, repo, or items"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Schema version check
    local version
    version=$(jq -r '.schema_version' "$plan_file")
    if [[ "$version" != "1" ]]; then
        echo "Unsupported schema version: $version"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Items must have required fields
    if ! jq -e '.items | all(.type and .number and .decision)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Items missing required fields: type, number, or decision"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate decision values
    local invalid_decisions
    invalid_decisions=$(jq -r '.items[] | select(.decision | IN("fix","skip","needs-info","closed") | not) | .decision' "$plan_file")
    if [[ -n "$invalid_decisions" ]]; then
        echo "Invalid decision values: $invalid_decisions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate gh_actions if present
    if jq -e '.gh_actions' "$plan_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! jq -e '.gh_actions | all(.op and .target)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
            echo "gh_actions missing required fields: op or target"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "Valid"
    return 0
}
```

## Plan Summary
```bash
summarize_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    jq -r '
        "Repository: \(.repo)",
        "Items reviewed: \(.items | length)",
        "  - Fixed: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "fix")] | length)",
        "  - Skipped: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "skip")] | length)",
        "  - Needs info: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "needs-info")] | length)",
        "Commits: \(.git.commits // [] | length)",
        "Tests: \(if .git.tests.ok then "PASS" else "FAIL/NOT RUN" end)",
        "gh_actions pending: \(.gh_actions // [] | length)"
    ' "$plan_file"
}
```

## Testing
- Validate correct plan passes
- Validate missing fields rejected
- Validate invalid decisions rejected
- Parse all field types correctly
- Handle optional fields gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema documented with all fields
- [ ] Validation function catches all errors
- [ ] Example plans provided for testing
- [ ] Summary function works
- [ ] Optional fields handled correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:01.268595285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented validate_review_plan(), summarize_review_plan(), and get_review_plan_json_summary() functions in ru script. Added 14 unit tests in scripts/test_unit_review_plan.sh. All tests pass."}
 {"id":"bd-kvu5","title":"[EPIC] Error Handling & Recovery","description":"# Error Handling & Recovery

## Error Type Mapping

| Scenario | ntm Exit | ntm Error Code | ru Exit | ru Behavior |
|----------|----------|----------------|---------|-------------|
| ntm not installed | N/A | N/A | 3 | Log install command |
| tmux not installed | 2 | DEPENDENCY_MISSING | 3 | Log install advice |
| Session already exists | 1 | RESOURCE_BUSY | Skip | Kill existing, retry |
| Spawn timeout | 1 | TIMEOUT | Skip repo | Log, continue |
| Send failed | 1 | INTERNAL_ERROR | Skip repo | Log, cleanup, continue |
| Wait timeout | 1 | TIMEOUT | Skip repo | Log, cleanup, continue |
| Agent error detected | 3 | (state-based) | Skip repo | Log, cleanup, continue |
| Rate limit detected | 3 | (pattern match) | Pause | Global backoff, retry |
| Network error | 1 | (pattern match) | Skip repo | Log, continue |
| Interrupted (Ctrl+C) | N/A | N/A | 5 | Save state, cleanup |
| Preflight failed | N/A | N/A | Skip repo | Record reason, continue |
| Validation failed | N/A | N/A | Skip repo | Block commit, preserve plan |

## ntm Error Codes
| Error Code | Meaning | ru Exit Code |
|------------|---------|--------------|
| SESSION_NOT_FOUND | Session doesn't exist | 3 |
| PANE_NOT_FOUND | Pane index invalid | 3 |
| INVALID_FLAG | Bad CLI arguments | 4 |
| TIMEOUT | Wait exceeded timeout | 1 |
| INTERNAL_ERROR | Unexpected Go error | 3 |
| PERMISSION_DENIED | File/tmux permissions | 3 |
| RESOURCE_BUSY | Session locked | 1 |
| DEPENDENCY_MISSING | tmux not installed | 3 |
| NOT_IMPLEMENTED | Feature not ready | 4 |

## Rate Limit Recovery
- Detect via ntm activity (rate_limited:true) or pattern match
- Trigger global backoff (shared across all workers)
- Exponential backoff with jitter
- Max wait: 10 minutes

## Crash Recovery
- Detect via ntm wait exit code 3 or ERROR state
- Capture pane output before cleanup
- Option to preserve session for debugging

## Orphan Session Cleanup
cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() kills all sessions matching pattern:
- ru_sweep_*_$$* or ru_sweep_*_$$_*
- Respects --keep-sessions flag

## Trap Handlers
trap 'cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions; save_agent_sweep_state "interrupted"' INT TERM

## Partial Success Handling
- Continue processing remaining repos on individual failures
- Aggregate results at end
- Exit code reflects worst case (0=all ok, 1=some failed, 2=conflicts)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:47:04.091975853-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:47:04.091975853-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kvu5","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:20.171369182-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Requires real git repos (depends on git operation tests).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:53:35.374767032-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T11:12:19.899504837-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T11:12:19.899504837-05:00","close_reason":"Added 4 tests for get_main_repo_path_from_worktree() to test_unit_worktree.sh. -{"id":"bd-kzzl","title":"Write comprehensive preflight check tests","description":"# Comprehensive Preflight Check Tests

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_preflight_checks.sh

## Purpose
Verify all 13 preflight conditions work correctly. Each check must pass before invoking the agent.

## Preflight Conditions to Test (13 total)

1. **is_git_repo** - Directory is a git repository
2. **git_email_configured** - user.email is set (ADDED)
3. **git_name_configured** - user.name is set (ADDED)
4. **not_shallow_clone** - Repository is not a shallow clone (ADDED)
5. **no_dirty_submodules** - Submodules are clean (ADDED)
6. **no_rebase_in_progress** - .git/rebase-apply or rebase-merge absent
7. **no_merge_in_progress** - .git/MERGE_HEAD absent
8. **no_cherry_pick_in_progress** - .git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD absent
9. **not_detached_HEAD** - symbolic-ref returns valid branch
10. **has_upstream** - @{u} exists (when push strategy != none)
11. **not_diverged** - not both ahead AND behind upstream
12. **no_unmerged_paths** - no merge conflicts
13. **diff_check_clean** - no whitespace errors
14. **reasonable_untracked_count** - not too many untracked files

## Test Functions

### Git Identity Tests (NEW)
```bash
test_preflight_git_identity() {
    log_test_start "Git identity configuration check"

    local repo=$(mktemp -d)
    git init "$repo" >/dev/null 2>&1

    log_verbose "Testing with no identity"
    git -C "$repo" config --unset user.email 2>/dev/null || true
    repo_preflight_check "$repo" && fail "Should fail without email"
    assert_equals "git_email_not_configured" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"

    log_verbose "Testing with email but no name"
    git -C "$repo" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$repo" config --unset user.name 2>/dev/null || true
    repo_preflight_check "$repo" && fail "Should fail without name"
    assert_equals "git_name_not_configured" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"

    rm -rf "$repo"
    log_success "Git identity tests passed"
}
```

### Shallow Clone Test (NEW)
```bash
test_preflight_shallow_clone() {
    log_test_start "Shallow clone detection"

    # Create a repo with history
    local origin=$(mktemp -d)
    git init "$origin" >/dev/null 2>&1
    git -C "$origin" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$origin" config user.name "Test"
    echo "file" > "$origin/file.txt"
    git -C "$origin" add . && git -C "$origin" commit -m "init" >/dev/null
    echo "file2" > "$origin/file2.txt"
    git -C "$origin" add . && git -C "$origin" commit -m "second" >/dev/null

    # Shallow clone
    local shallow=$(mktemp -d)
    git clone --depth 1 "file://$origin" "$shallow" >/dev/null 2>&1

    log_verbose "Testing shallow clone detection"
    [[ -f "$shallow/.git/shallow" ]] || fail "Test setup: should be shallow"
    repo_preflight_check "$shallow" && fail "Should reject shallow clone"
    assert_equals "shallow_clone" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"

    rm -rf "$origin" "$shallow"
    log_success "Shallow clone tests passed"
}
```

### Submodule Test (NEW)
```bash
test_preflight_dirty_submodules() {
    log_test_start "Dirty submodule detection"

    # Setup: parent repo with submodule
    local submod=$(mktemp -d)
    local parent=$(mktemp -d)

    git init "$submod" >/dev/null 2>&1
    git -C "$submod" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$submod" config user.name "Test"
    echo "sub" > "$submod/sub.txt"
    git -C "$submod" add . && git -C "$submod" commit -m "init" >/dev/null

    git init "$parent" >/dev/null 2>&1
    git -C "$parent" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$parent" config user.name "Test"
    git -C "$parent" submodule add "file://$submod" sub >/dev/null 2>&1
    git -C "$parent" commit -m "add submodule" >/dev/null

    log_verbose "Testing clean submodule"
    repo_preflight_check "$parent" || fail "Should pass with clean submodule"

    log_verbose "Testing dirty submodule"
    echo "dirty" > "$parent/sub/dirty.txt"
    repo_preflight_check "$parent" && fail "Should reject dirty submodule"
    assert_equals "dirty_submodules" "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"

    rm -rf "$submod" "$parent"
    log_success "Submodule tests passed"
}
```

## Parallel Preflight Tests
```bash
test_parallel_preflight() {
    log_test_start "Parallel preflight execution"

    # Create mix of valid and invalid repos
    local valid1=$(mktemp -d)
    local valid2=$(mktemp -d)
    local invalid=$(mktemp -d)

    setup_valid_repo "$valid1"
    setup_valid_repo "$valid2"
    # Don't init invalid - it's not a git repo

    local repos=("$valid1" "$valid2" "$invalid")

    run_parallel_preflight repos

    assert_equals 2 "${#repos[@]}" "Should have 2 valid repos"
    assert_contains "${repos[*]}" "$valid1" "Should include valid1"
    assert_contains "${repos[*]}" "$valid2" "Should include valid2"

    rm -rf "$valid1" "$valid2" "$invalid"
    log_success "Parallel preflight tests passed"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 preflight conditions have dedicated tests
- [ ] Tests create realistic scenarios (actual git repos)
- [ ] Failure messages include skip reasons
- [ ] Tests clean up temp directories
- [ ] Parallel preflight tested with mix of repos
- [ ] Verbose logging shows test progress

 {"id":"bd-ldyv","title":"Implement quality gates framework (tests/lint before push)","description":"Task: Implement Quality Gates Framework

Purpose
-------
Run tests and lint before allowing push, ensuring code quality and
preventing broken commits from reaching the repository.

Background: Defense in Depth
----------------------------
AI can make mistakes. Quality gates catch:
- Syntax errors
- Test failures
- Lint violations
- Security issues (secrets in code)
- Build failures

Gate Types
----------

1. Test Gate
   Run project test suite
   Command: auto-detect or configured
   Failure: blocks push

2. Lint Gate
   Run linters/formatters
   Command: configured per-repo
   Failure: blocks push

3. Secret Scan Gate
   Check for exposed secrets
   Tool: gitleaks or regex fallback
   Failure: blocks push with warning

4. Build Gate (optional)
   Verify project builds
   Command: configured
   Failure: blocks push

Implementation
--------------

run_quality_gates()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Load per-repo policy
  local repo_id=$(jq -r .repo "$plan_file")
  local policy="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
  [[ -f "$policy" ]] && source "$policy"
  
  # Run lint if configured
  if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD" ]]; then
    log_step "Running lint: $REVIEW_LINT_CMD"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD"); then
      log_error "Lint failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  # Run tests
  run_test_gate "$wt_path" || return 1
  
  # Secret scanning
  run_secret_scan "$wt_path" || return 2  # 2 = needs review
  
  log_success "Quality gates passed"
  return 0

run_test_gate()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local test_cmd="${REVIEW_TEST_CMD:-}"
  
  # Auto-detect if not configured
  if [[ -z "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "$wt_path/Makefile" ]] && grep -q "^test:" "$wt_path/Makefile"; then
      test_cmd="make test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/package.json" ]]; then
      test_cmd="npm test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="cargo test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/setup.py" || -f "$wt_path/pyproject.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="pytest"
    fi
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    log_step "Running tests: $test_cmd"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$test_cmd"); then
      log_error "Tests failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

run_secret_scan()
  local wt_path="$1"
  
  if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
    log_step "Scanning for secrets with gitleaks"
    if \! gitleaks detect --source "$wt_path" --no-git; then
      log_error "Secrets detected in changes"
      return 1
    fi
  else
    # Regex fallback
    if git -C "$wt_path" diff HEAD~1..HEAD | \
       grep -qiE "password\s*=|api.?key\s*=|secret\s*=|token\s*="; then
      log_warn "Potential secrets detected"
      return 2  # Needs human review
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

Policy Configuration
--------------------
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:

REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS=true
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true

Gate Results in Plan
--------------------
Update plan with gate results:
{
  "git": {
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  }
}

Testing
-------
- Verify auto-detection works
- Verify configured commands run
- Verify failures block push
- Verify secret scan catches patterns
- Test with missing tools

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Test gate runs before push
- [ ] Lint gate runs if configured
- [ ] Secret scan warns on patterns
- [ ] Failures block push with message
- [ ] Per-repo policies work
- [ ] Auto-detection works for common projects","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:44.686321504-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:21:11.827464852-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:21:11.827464852-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented quality gates framework with 8 functions: detect_test_command, detect_lint_command, run_test_gate, run_lint_gate, run_secret_scan, run_quality_gates, update_plan_with_gates. Supports auto-detection for Makefile, npm, Cargo, Python, Go, shell. Integrates with per-repo policies.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.491001528-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-cutq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:10:25.431274346-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-m3a5","title":"Write unit tests for ntm driver functions","description":"# Unit Tests for ntm Driver Functions

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_unit_ntm_driver.sh

## Tests to Implement

### JSON Parsing Tests
```bash
test_json_get_field() {
    local json="{\"success\":true,\"error\":\"test error\"}"
    local result
    result=$(json_get_field "$json" "error")
    assert_equals "test error" "$result" "Should extract error field"
}

test_json_is_success() {
    json_is_success "{\"success\":true}"
    assert_equals 0 $? "Should return 0 for success:true"
    
    json_is_success "{\"success\":false}"
    assert_equals 1 $? "Should return 1 for success:false"
}

test_json_escape() {
    local result=$(json_escape "quote\"and\\backslash")
    assert_equals "quote\\\"and\\\\backslash" "$result"
}
```

### ntm Check Tests
```bash
test_ntm_check_available_not_installed() {
    PATH="/empty" ntm_check_available
    assert_equals 1 $? "Should return 1 when ntm not installed"
}
```

### Preflight Tests
```bash
test_has_uncommitted_changes() {
    local test_repo=$(mktemp -d)
    git -C "$test_repo" init
    
    has_uncommitted_changes "$test_repo"
    assert_equals 1 $? "Clean repo should return 1"
    
    touch "$test_repo/newfile"
    has_uncommitted_changes "$test_repo"
    assert_equals 0 $? "Dirty repo should return 0"
    
    rm -rf "$test_repo"
}
```

### Security Tests
```bash
test_is_file_denied() {
    source "$RU_SCRIPT"
    
    is_file_denied ".env"
    assert_equals 0 $? ".env should be denied"
    
    is_file_denied "src/main.py"
    assert_equals 1 $? "src/main.py should be allowed"
}
```

+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# AGENT-SWEEP RELEASE WORKFLOW DETECTION
+#
+# Determine if a repository should have release automation (Phase 3).
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+# Check if repo has release workflow configured.
+# Checks in order: per-repo config, user config, gh API, workflow files.
+# Usage: has_release_workflow /path/to/repo
+# Returns: 0 if release workflow detected, 1 otherwise
+has_release_workflow() {
+    local repo_path="${1:-}"
+    [[ -z "$repo_path" || ! -d "$repo_path" ]] && return 1
+
+    local workflows_dir="$repo_path/.github/workflows"
+
+    # 1. Check explicit per-repo config first (highest priority)
+    local repo_config="$repo_path/.ru/agent-sweep.conf"
+    if [[ -f "$repo_config" ]]; then
+        local AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY=""
+        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
+        source "$repo_config" 2>/dev/null
+        case "${AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY:-}" in
+            never) return 1 ;;
+            tag-only|gh-release|auto) return 0 ;;
+        esac
+    fi
+
+    # 2. Check user-level per-repo config
+    local repo_name
+    repo_name=$(basename "$repo_path")
+    local user_config="${RU_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/ru}/agent-sweep.d/${repo_name}.conf"
+    if [[ -f "$user_config" ]]; then
+        local AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY=""
+        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
+        source "$user_config" 2>/dev/null
+        case "${AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY:-}" in
+            never) return 1 ;;
+            tag-only|gh-release|auto) return 0 ;;
+        esac
+    fi
+
+    # 3. Use gh API if available (checks remote for release workflows)
+    if command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
+        local remote_url
+        remote_url=$(git -C "$repo_path" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null)
+        if [[ -n "$remote_url" ]]; then
+            # Extract owner/repo from URL
+            local repo_spec
+            repo_spec=$(echo "$remote_url" | sed -E 's#.*[:/]([^/]+/[^/]+)(\.git)?$#\1#')
+            if [[ -n "$repo_spec" ]]; then
+                # Check for release-related workflows
+                if gh workflow list -R "$repo_spec" 2>/dev/null | grep -qiE "release|deploy|publish"; then
+                    return 0
+                fi
+            fi
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # 4. Fallback: check local workflow files for release patterns
+    [[ -d "$workflows_dir" ]] || return 1
+
+    # Look for release-related triggers or jobs in workflow files
+    if compgen -G "$workflows_dir/*.yml" >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
+       compgen -G "$workflows_dir/*.yaml" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        # Check for common release patterns
+        if grep -riqE "(on:[[:space:]]*release|tags:|workflow_dispatch:|create:[[:space:]]*tags)" \
+           "$workflows_dir"/*.yml "$workflows_dir"/*.yaml 2>/dev/null; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+        # Check for release job names
+        if grep -riqE "(release|publish|deploy)" \
+           "$workflows_dir"/*.yml "$workflows_dir"/*.yaml 2>/dev/null; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Get the release strategy for a repo.
+# Returns: "never", "auto", "tag-only", or "gh-release"
+get_release_strategy() {
+    local repo_path="${1:-}"
+    [[ -z "$repo_path" || ! -d "$repo_path" ]] && { echo "never"; return; }
+
+    # Check per-repo config
+    local repo_config="$repo_path/.ru/agent-sweep.conf"
+    if [[ -f "$repo_config" ]]; then
+        local AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY=""
+        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
+        source "$repo_config" 2>/dev/null
+        if [[ -n "$AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY" ]]; then
+            echo "$AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY"
+            return
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Check user config
+    local repo_name
+    repo_name=$(basename "$repo_path")
+    local user_config="${RU_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/ru}/agent-sweep.d/${repo_name}.conf"
+    if [[ -f "$user_config" ]]; then
+        local AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY=""
+        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
+        source "$user_config" 2>/dev/null
+        if [[ -n "$AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY" ]]; then
+            echo "$AGENT_SWEEP_RELEASE_STRATEGY"
+            return
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Default: auto if has release workflow, never otherwise
+    if has_release_workflow "$repo_path"; then
+        echo "auto"
+    else
+        echo "never"
+    fi
+}
+
 # Load per-repo agent-sweep configuration.
 # Usage: load_repo_agent_config /path/to/repo
 # Returns: 0 on success (uses defaults if no config found), 1 on invalid args
